body.text: This monograph establishes the Minimum Enclosing Structure as a Daubert-ready evidence base for legal proceedings, providing mathematical proof of structural integrity in cognitive systems.
Building on the Tetrahedron Protocol, this work extends the mathematical framework to minimum enclosing structures for legal validation.
Extension of K₄ mesh principles to minimum enclosing structures
Provides admissible evidence for Johnson v. Johnson (2025CV936) and similar cases involving cognitive liberty and neurodivergent rights.
